_doc: Registry of models in flight + current champion. Updated when a challenger is promoted. Read by predictor/scripts/live_run.py to decide which model takes the live paper position.
champion_reason_initial: Used for Run 002 (NYC LOWT bin B50.5, WIN +$56.16, Brier model 0.0213 vs Brier kalshi_mid 0.1296). The first model deployed live in Phase 1.
promotion_rule: A challenger is promoted to champion when (1) its source training run has `promotable: true` in run.json (n_distinct_test_split_values >= 3), AND (2) its rolling-mean Brier over the most recent N>=10 resolved trades is strictly lower than the current champion's, AND (3) a 1-sided binomial sign test on per-trade Brier wins yields p < 0.10. Promotion is recorded in `history[]` below. Editors MUST reject any promotion attempt sourced from a run with `promotable: false`.

models.name: vendor_ensemble
models.description: Mean of 4 vendor probabilities (ECMWF + GraphCast + GFS + JMA) blended with climatology by horizon. Trained-free: takes vendor outputs directly, no parameter fitting.

models.name: learned_v2
models.description: sklearn LogisticRegression(L2, C=1.0) over 11 features (FEATURES_V2: 5 forecast-derived + 6 static geographic). Trained on resolved Kalshi events with chronological 70/30 split. See predictor/runs_learning/<ts>/run.json for fitted state.

models.name: kalshi_mid_baseline
models.description: Just the Kalshi market mid-price as the prediction. The lower bar: if a model can't beat this on average, it has no edge over the market itself.
